page: Amazon Web Services r8i.96xlarge is a memory-optimized server powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors using the x86_64 architecture. It features 384 virtual CPUs across 192 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 3072.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It utilizes the Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and does not include any GPUs. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark results place this server in the top tier for multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations. The Intel Xeon processor includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions, which contribute to top-tier performance in Passmark extended instructions and cryptography tests. Memory read bandwidth reaches up to 1125.32 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, and Redis SET operations exceed 508 million operations per second. While prompt processing for large language models is strong, text generation speeds are limited due to the absence of dedicated GPU acceleration.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the r8i.96xlarge offers high resource density for memory-bound workloads, reducing the need for multi-node clustering. However, the lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may introduce latency tradeoffs. This server is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise applications, high-throughput web serving, and heavy multi-threaded computing tasks that require massive memory capacity alongside high-core-count parallel processing.
description: Amazon Web Services r8i.96xlarge is a memory-optimized server designed for high-throughput, memory-intensive workloads. Powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors on the x86_64 architecture, it delivers 384 vCPUs, 192 physical cores, and 3072.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory. Running on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance features a 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and outstanding database operations, including over 508 million Redis SET operations per second. The integration of Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions accelerates matrix operations, though LLM text generation remains limited by the lack of a GPU. This server is highly efficient for large in-memory databases, intensive data processing, and enterprise applications requiring massive memory capacity and parallel computing power.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.96xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 384 vCPUs, 3072 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for large-scale in-memory databases and parallel computing.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.96xlarge is a memory-optimized server with 384 vCPUs, 3072 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized platform featuring three terabytes of DDR5 RAM and three hundred eighty-four virtual cores for massive parallel workloads.
bullet_points: 384 vCPUs and 192 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors
3072 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction support for accelerated cryptography and matrix math
Best suited for large-scale in-memory databases and heavy multi-threaded enterprise workloads
